items.title: Execution Replay & Time Travel
items.description: Let users replay any past bot execution step-by-step, pausing at each log line to inspect state. Solves the pain of debugging why an automation failed days ago when logs have scrolled away or context is lost.

items.title: Bot Template Marketplace
items.description: A curated library of pre-built bot templates (security audits, PR reviews, dependency updates, release notes) that teams can one-click install and customize. Eliminates blank-slate problem when teams first adopt the platform.

items.title: Visual DAG Workflow Composer
items.description: Drag-and-drop canvas where users connect bots into multi-step pipelines with conditional branches and parallel execution. Unlocks complex automations (e.g., run security scan → if findings exist, open Jira ticket → notify Slack) without writing code.

items.title: AI-Assisted Prompt Editor
items.description: An inline editor for bot prompt templates that suggests improvements, warns about ambiguous instructions, and lets users test prompts against sample payloads before saving. Reduces trial-and-error when crafting effective automation prompts.

items.title: AI Execution Cost Tracker
items.description: Track estimated token usage and cost per bot execution, per team, per time period, with budget alerts. Solves the problem of surprise AI spend at month-end with no visibility into which bots or teams are burning the most tokens.

items.title: Multi-Provider Fallback Chains
items.description: Configure ordered fallback chains (Claude → Gemini → Codex) so executions automatically retry on the next provider if one fails or rate-limits. Maximizes automation uptime without manual intervention.

items.title: Slack / Teams Notification Hub
items.description: Route bot execution results, failures, and summaries to configurable Slack channels or MS Teams. Engineers need execution outcomes where they already work, not just in a dashboard they have to actively check.

items.title: PR Comment Command Triggers
items.description: Allow bots to be triggered by PR comments like /review or /security-scan. Makes automation feel native to the GitHub workflow engineers already use rather than requiring a separate dashboard visit.

items.title: Execution Diff Viewer
items.description: When a bot makes code changes (e.g., auto-fix, refactor), show a unified diff of before/after within the platform alongside the execution log. Removes the need to switch to GitHub to see what the bot actually changed.

items.title: Team Activity Feed
items.description: A real-time feed showing all recent bot executions, trigger events, and configuration changes across a team. Solves the coordination problem of teams not knowing what automations ran or what their teammates changed.

items.title: Conditional Trigger Rules Engine
items.description: Add filter conditions to triggers (e.g., only fire PR review bot if PR touches files in src/, or only run security audit if branch is main). Reduces noise and wasted executions from bots firing on irrelevant events.

items.title: Execution Analytics Dashboard
items.description: Charts showing execution frequency, success/failure rates, average duration, and cost trends over time per bot and team. Helps teams identify which bots provide value and which are flaky or expensive.

items.title: Bot Health Score
items.description: Automatically compute a health score per bot based on recent success rate, execution time trends, and error patterns. Surfaces degrading bots before they become a problem, without requiring users to manually monitor logs.

items.title: Secret Vault Integration
items.description: Connect to HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, or 1Password to inject secrets into bot prompts and environment variables at execution time. Eliminates the unsafe practice of hardcoding credentials in prompt templates.

items.title: Scheduled Trigger Calendar View
items.description: A calendar UI showing when scheduled bots will run, have run, and their outcomes. Makes it easy to see if an important weekly audit ran on time or was missed, without digging through logs.

items.title: Bot Dry Run Mode
items.description: Run a bot with a real payload but intercept the AI call and return a preview of the prompt that would be sent, without executing. Lets engineers validate prompt rendering and trigger matching safely before going live.

items.title: Cross-Repository Bot Coverage
items.description: Configure a single bot to watch multiple GitHub repositories with per-repo overrides. Solves the tedious problem of duplicating bot configurations across dozens of repos in a large engineering org.

items.title: Execution Approval Gates
items.description: Require human approval before a bot applies changes or posts results, with a one-click approve/reject UI in the dashboard or Slack. Gives teams control over high-stakes automations without removing the efficiency benefit.

items.title: Role-Based Access Control
items.description: Fine-grained permissions so team members can view/run bots but not edit them, and admins can manage all resources. Prevents accidental misconfiguration in shared team environments.

items.title: Jira / Linear Auto-Issue Creation
items.description: Let bots automatically create Jira or Linear tickets from their findings (e.g., security audit findings become tracked issues). Closes the loop between AI discovery and engineering follow-through.

items.title: Prompt Version History
items.description: Track every change to a bot's prompt template with diffs, who changed it, and when. Let users roll back to any previous version. Fixes the problem of broken bots with no way to know what changed or revert safely.

items.title: One-Click Bot Cloning
items.description: Clone an existing bot with all its triggers and configuration as a starting point for a new one. Saves significant time when creating similar bots for different repos or slightly different use cases.

items.title: Full-Text Execution Log Search
items.description: Search across all historical execution logs with filters by bot, date range, status, and keyword. Makes it possible to answer questions like 'has any bot flagged this file in the last 30 days?' without scrolling through individual runs.

items.title: Agent Persistent Memory / Context
items.description: Let bots maintain a rolling context window of their past findings (e.g., previous security issues, prior PR review patterns) that gets injected into future executions. Makes automation smarter over time instead of starting from scratch every run.

items.title: Webhook Payload Inspector
items.description: A live inspector that captures incoming webhook payloads, shows exactly what was received, and traces which bots were matched and why. Eliminates the guesswork when a trigger fires unexpectedly or doesn't fire at all.

items.title: Execution Rate Limiting & Queuing
items.description: Configure max concurrent executions per bot or team with automatic queuing for overflow. Prevents bot storms (e.g., 50 PRs merged simultaneously) from overwhelming AI provider rate limits or system resources.

items.title: GitHub App One-Click Installation
items.description: Publish Agented as a GitHub App so teams install it on their org with one click, automatically configuring webhooks and permissions. Removes the friction of manual webhook setup that blocks initial adoption.

items.title: Bot Test Suite with Fixture Payloads
items.description: Let users define a set of test payloads (mock PR events, webhook bodies) and expected bot behaviors, then run them on demand or in CI. Gives engineers confidence that bot changes don't break existing behavior.

items.title: Execution Tagging & Annotations
items.description: Allow engineers to tag executions (e.g., 'false positive', 'resolved', 'escalated') and add notes. Creates an institutional memory of how automation findings were handled, useful for audits and retrospectives.

items.title: A/B Model Comparison Mode
items.description: Run the same prompt against two different AI models simultaneously and display results side-by-side. Helps teams choose the best model for each use case with data rather than guesswork.

items.title: Guided Onboarding Wizard
items.description: A step-by-step wizard that walks new users from account creation to their first working bot in under 10 minutes — connecting GitHub, configuring a trigger, and running a test execution. Solves the high drop-off when users can't figure out where to start.

items.title: Execution Report Export
items.description: Export execution summaries as PDF, Markdown, or CSV for sharing with stakeholders outside the platform (e.g., weekly security audit report to a CISO). Makes AI-generated insights actionable beyond the engineering team.

items.title: Bot Output as Trigger Input
items.description: Use the structured output of one bot as the trigger payload for another, creating native bot-to-bot pipelines. Enables compound workflows like: summarize PR → if summary flags risk → escalate to senior review bot.

items.title: Mobile-Friendly Execution Monitor
items.description: A responsive mobile view for monitoring bot execution status and receiving push notifications for failures. Engineering on-call rotations need to check automation health from phones, not just laptops.

items.title: Custom Metrics Webhook Egress
items.description: Emit structured execution metrics (timing, status, cost, model) to external observability platforms like Datadog, Grafana, or Honeycomb. Lets teams integrate AI automation observability into their existing monitoring stack.
